data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project with a failing test (`express` returns `10.00` but the test expects `15.00`). I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests automatically re-run when you save changes.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project with two tests. The second test (`CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15`) will fail because the `CalculateShipping` method returns `10.00m` for "express" but the test expects `15.00m`.

They want to use hot reload to iterate faster. I can set up `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests when files change.

Let me start it up for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-cd03a63fc2c441bab2324ea8375ce7d2 && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — **failed** (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is active and waiting for file changes. Whenever you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will automatically re-run. 

To fix the failing test, change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` in the `CalculateShipping` method (line 24). Want me to make that fix?
